title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Convenes World's Largest Longevity Festival This Week
description: Vitalist Bay 2026 returns to Berkeley's Lighthaven Campus May 14-17 with 60+ speakers across six themes — Biotech, Investors, Replacement, Longevity Science, AI x Bio, and Biostasis. Sessions slated to cover epigenetic reprogramming, vascular rejuvenation, female longevity, AI in defeating aging, and unlocking new funding pathways will set the agenda for capital allocation in H2 2026.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Sets New Bar — Inflammaging Becomes the Investable Theme
description: Friday's BioAge R&D Day delivered Phase 1 BGE-102 results with 86% median hsCRP reduction at 60 mg and 120 mg once-daily dosing. With AstraZeneca's tozorakimab Phase 3 sweep also fresh, NLRP3 inhibition and IL-33 antibodies have repositioned inflammaging as the cleanest near-term clinical thesis in longevity-adjacent biotech.

title: ER-100 Clears Regulators for First Age-Reversal Gene Therapy Trial in 2026
description: A global longevity biotech secured regulatory clearance for ER-100, a Phase 1 epigenetic-reprogramming gene therapy aimed at partially resetting biological age in retinal cells. The first cohort will be glaucoma patients, with preclinical animal data showing near-complete vision restoration within weeks — the most ambitious clinical milestone the partial-reprogramming field has reached to date.

title: Geroscience Pivots Hard From Observation to Intervention in 2026
description: American Federation for Aging Research's 2026 review documents the shift from observational aging research to intervention-grade trials targeting senescence, metabolism, and mTOR pathways. The TAME framework is now expanding to evaluate SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1R agonists alongside rapamycin as aging-targeting candidates, accelerating the convergence of metabolic and geroscience drug pipelines.

title: Senolytic Pipeline Three-Lane Reality Check Sharpens Investor Decisions
description: Reading the senolytics landscape across the past two weeks (Imperial GPX4 senolytics, Phase 2 fisetin, Seragon SRN-901, UNITY's localized programs) the field has clearly bifurcated: localized indications keep generating clean signal while systemic anti-aging claims remain unproven. Investors should price localized senolytic programs as standard biotech and treat broad lifespan claims as still preclinical.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Returns to Berkeley May 14-17
description: Largest longevity festival of the year returns to Lighthaven with 60+ speakers across six themes; sessions cover drug discovery R&D productivity, longevity policy and regulation, replacement medicine, and rebranding longevity for new funding pathways.

title: BioAge BGE-102 R&D Day: 86% Median hsCRP Reduction
description: BGE-102 Phase 1 across 60 mg and 120 mg once-daily cohorts; 87-93% of participants reached hsCRP below 2 mg/L. Therapeutic positioning spans ASCVD and retinal disease.

title: BioAge Q1 2026 Cash Runway Extended Into 2028
description: Q1 update extends cash runway into 2028; pipeline highlights long-acting injectable APJ agonists, an exclusive option for an APJ nanobody, and orally active APJ agonists for obesity, plus BGE-102 in Phase 2 prep.

title: University of Arizona Phase 3 Rapamycin Trial Officially Launches
description: 720-participant double-blind Phase 3 of weekly low-dose rapamycin in adults 65+ projected to run six years; co-primary endpoints are frailty transition and IL-6.

title: NOVOS Core Topline Trial Shows Vascular Aging Reversal
description: Six-month RCT in healthy adults 40+ showed NOVOS Core improved FMD by 2.9 percentage points, pulse-wave velocity by 1.18 m/s, and reduced systolic BP — magnitudes near or exceeding aerobic exercise effects.

title: Rapamycin News — May 2026 Clinical Studies in Aging
description: Monthly digest covers Everolimus Aging Study (NCT05835999), sirolimus aging biomarker trials, and a postmenopausal GLP-1 vascular-aging study (NCT07057310).

title: Healthy For Long — From Hype to Human Trials
description: Field guide profiling nine longevity startups crossing from animal data to human trials across seven distinct mechanisms — finally putting clinical readouts at the center of the narrative.

title: Harvard Reframes Cancer Genetics Through an Aging Lens
description: HMS researchers reframe how age-driven mutational landscapes shape cancer biology, strengthening the case that cancer and biological aging are overlapping rather than separable problems.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form profile of Altos Labs covers rat kidney rejuvenation, planned pig studies, and Life Biosciences' FDA-cleared human partial-reprogramming trial in optic neuropathies.

title: FightAging — STING Nitrosylation Drives Alzheimer Neuroinflammation
description: Scripps team shows S-nitrosylation of STING at cysteine 148 (SNO-STING) drives chronic neuroinflammation; blocking the modification protects synapses in Alzheimer mouse models.

title: FightAging — Young-Mouse FMT Reduces Liver Cancer in Aged Mice
description: FMT from young mice suppresses MDM2 and inflammation in old mice; zero of eight FMT-treated animals developed liver tumors versus two of eight controls.

title: Aging Research Shifts to Interventional with Geroscience Trials
source: American Federation for Aging Research / LinkedIn
description: 2026 review documents the rapid rise of geroscience-driven clinical trials and integration of aging biomarkers into interventional studies, with the TAME framework expanding to SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1R agonists.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Combo Extends Median Mouse Lifespan 33%
description: Combination of urolithin A, quercetin, NR, ALA and SRN-820 increased median mouse lifespan by 33% and reduced frailty, providing a translational template for multi-target geroprotector stacks.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets a Key Vulnerability in Ageing Cells
description: Imperial researchers identified GPX4-targeting chloroacetamides that selectively kill senescent cells via ferroptosis (Nature Cell Biology).

title: Mesoblast Advances CAR-MSC Platform; 300-Patient Phase 3 Enrolled
description: Mesoblast (ASX:MSB) finished enrolling its 300-patient Phase 3 of rexlemestrocel-L in chronic low back pain and licensed Mayo Clinic CAR technology for IBD and CD19 lupus nephritis programs.

title: Sinclair: Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Days Away
description: At Abundance360, David Sinclair said the first human partial-reprogramming trial using three Yamanaka factors will dose a patient's eye to test reversal of vision loss within weeks.

title: Russia Develops Anti-Aging Vaccine Targeting RAGE
description: Deputy science minister confirmed a gene therapy candidate blocking the RAGE receptor under Russia's New Technologies for Health Preservation program; production targeted 2028-2030.

title: Longevity Biotech Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026 Financing
description: DLT tracker recorded $3.74B raised across 49 financings in Q1 2026, up 56% YoY, concentrated in epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health.

title: Wedbush — Longevity Trade is a $120B Market
description: Wedbush analyst note frames longevity as a $120B addressable market following FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and Longeveron's Phase 2b laromestrocel data.

title: Insilico Forms Industry-First Longevity Board
description: HKEX-listed Insilico Medicine (3696) launched the industry's first Longevity Board to oversee aging-research strategy across life models, biomarkers, and target identification.

title: AstraZeneca Tozorakimab Wins Third Phase 3 COPD Trial
description: MIRANDA showed statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of standard inhaled care, completing a clean three-trial Phase 3 sweep for AZ's IL-33 antibody.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Trial Finds Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 in sedentary older adults found smaller red cells, more platelets, higher alkaline phosphatase, higher HbA1c, and higher LDL versus placebo — a counterweight to rapamycin enthusiasm.

title: Multivitamins Modestly Slow Epigenetic Aging in COSMOS RCT
description: 958-participant ancillary study showed two years of multivitamin-multimineral use slowed all five tested epigenetic clocks, with statistically significant slowing in PCGrimAge and PCPhenoAge.

title: Fisetin Phase 2 Trial Targets Frailty in Breast Cancer Survivors
description: Phase 2 RCT tests oral fisetin in postmenopausal breast cancer survivors after neo/adjuvant chemo to evaluate whether senolytic clearance mitigates physical-function decline.

title: PRecision gerOMedicinE 8-Week Multimodal Trial
description: NCT07451496 evaluates an 8-week personalized geromedicine intervention combining lifestyle modification and targeted supplementation in adults 50-80 to improve biological and clinical markers of aging.
